steps.title: Identify the initial height and decay pattern
steps.content: The ball is dropped from 22 feet. After each bounce, the maximum height is 85% of the previous maximum height. We need to find h(n), the height after n ground hits.

steps.title: Build the formula for the geometric sequence
steps.content:
Each bounce multiplies the height by 0.85:
  • After bounce 1: $h(1) = 22 \times 0.85$
  • After bounce 2: $h(2) = 22 \times 0.85 \times 0.85 = 22(0.85)^2$
  • After bounce n: $h(n) = 22(0.85)^n$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
